perturbation.original_text: Each Party hereby agrees to fully comply with all applicable securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party hereby agrees to substantially comply with all applicable securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: The original text demands *full* compliance with securities laws. The modified text introduces the concept of *substantial* compliance. This is an in-text contradiction and weakens the obligation, creating uncertainty about what constitutes adequate compliance and directly contradicting the original commitment to 'fully' comply with all laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Applicable Securities Laws . Each Party hereby acknowledges that some or all of the Confidential Information is likely to constitute material, non-public information. Each Party hereby represents and warrants that it is aware (and that it shall advise its Representatives who are not already aware) that United States and other applicable securities laws prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning a publicly traded company or entity from purchasing or selling securities of such company or entity, or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. Each Party hereby agrees to substantially comply with all applicable securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'fully comply', while the changed text introduces 'substantially comply'. The contradicted text also states 'Each Party hereby agrees to substantially comply with all applicable securities laws.' The original text in conjunction with the contradicted text would cause one to assume that both parties are to fully comply. The changed text undermines the original intent by introducing the concept of 'substantial compliance,' creating an ambiguity and weakening the obligation. This directly contradicts the implicit understanding of full compliance, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, and that it shall cause its affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s CEO: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The original standstill clause requires invitation by the *Board of Directors*. The modification changes this to the *CEO*. This creates an in-text contradiction about who has the authority to invite the other party to engage in otherwise prohibited activities, undermining the clarity of the standstill provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill . For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, and that it shall cause its affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s CEO: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that an invitation from the Board of Directors is required to waive the standstill provision. The changed text alters this requirement, specifying that the CEO can provide the invitation instead. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text which specifies the CEO has the authority. Since both the changed and contradicted text agree with each other, and the original disagrees, the change introduces a conflict regarding who can authorize the waiver, satisfying the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction" and triggering a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A breaching Party shall reimburse the other Party for all reasonable costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, incurred by the other Party in the event it successfully enforces the obligations of the breaching Party or its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: A breaching Party shall reimburse the other Party for costs directly related to the breach, excluding attorneys’ fees, incurred by the other Party in the event it successfully enforces the obligations of the breaching Party or its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the breaching party shall reimburse *all reasonable costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees*. The modification states that the breaching party will reimburse costs *excluding attorneys' fees*. This is a direct in-text contradiction regarding the reimbursement of attorneys' fees, creating uncertainty about the extent of financial responsibility in case of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Each Party acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other Party could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, each Party, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. A breaching Party shall reimburse the other Party for costs directly related to the breach, excluding attorneys’ fees, incurred by the other Party in the event it successfully enforces the obligations of the breaching Party or its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that a breaching party shall reimburse all costs, including attorneys' fees. The changed text explicitly excludes attorneys' fees. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the same exclusion of attorney's fees, but implies that it used to include them because the original language of the text does include them. This is a clear inconsistency, therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Except with regard to paragraph 10, this Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'Except with regard to paragraph 10, this Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof.' The modification states 'This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof.'. This removes the term and obligation of paragraph 10 lasting more than 2 years. This is a direct in-text contradiction, creating uncertainty about how long paragraph 10 shall last for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term . This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof. [Remainder of Page Intentionally Left Blank]
perturbation.justification: The original text, 'Except with regard to paragraph 10, this Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof,' explicitly carves out an exception for paragraph 10, implying it could extend beyond the two-year term. The changed text, 'This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof,' removes this exception, setting a definitive two-year expiration for the entire agreement, including paragraph 10. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates an inconsistency regarding the duration of paragraph 10's obligations, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text, 'Term . This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the date hereof,' reinforces the two-year term. As such, the changed text directly creates a strong contradiction with the initial agreement, adhering to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
